title: Robust Canadian Bean Burrito
description: A bitter and juicy Canadian dish featuring a flour tortilla, this crowd-pleaser is a satvik-friendly option that is perfect for any meal, made with pressure-cooked beans and wrapped in a large flour tortilla.
instructions: Rinse the kidney beans and black beans and soak them in water for at least 8 hours.
Drain and rinse the soaked beans and add them to a pressure cooker with 4 cups of water.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground chili powder, and salt to the pressure cooker.
Close the lid of the pressure cooker and cook the beans for 20 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally from the pressure cooker.
Warm the large flour tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20 seconds.
Assemble the burritos by spooning the cooked bean mixture onto the center of a tortilla and topping with optional shredded cheese.
Fold the bottom edge of the tortilla up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the burrito up tightly.
Repeat the assembly process with the remaining tortillas and filling.
Serve the burritos hot and enjoy.
